question: Looking at the US GDP map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: West Virginia and Oklahoma have similar GDPs.
options.B: Florida’s GDP is far higher than California’s.
options.C: The GDP gap between West Virginia and Oklahoma might exceed that between Tennessee and Florida.
options.D: The GDP differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: West Virginia (216.8$B) and Oklahoma (319.2$B) have a 102.4$B GDP difference, while Tennessee (49.9$B) and Florida (50.1$B) have only a 0.2$B difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups West Virginia and Oklahoma into the same color bin (200-400$B range), making their large difference appear similar, while Tennessee and Florida appear more different due to different color bins.
